title: 50 Years On – Florence remembers the Flood of 1966
highlights: # 50 Years On – Florence remembers the Flood of 1966 On this day, the 4th of November in 1966, Florence was literally underwater as the worst flood in the city’s history caused major damage and destruction. Our local fruit and vegetable shop’s owner, Simone sent me some amazing photos of  ...    5,000 families were left homeless by the storm, and 6,000 stores were forced out of business. Approximately 600,000 tons of mud, rubble and sewage severely damaged or destroyed numerous collections of the written work and fine art for which Florence is famous. What the world mourned was the treasure damaged or destroyed: Etruscan art, ancient armor, musical instruments,  ...    Thousands of flood-damaged art works have been restored. Two decades later, thousands more still await restoration. The single greatest loss, art historians agree, was to Cimabue's crucifix, painted on wood around 1280. It had been moved from an upper floor of the Uffizi Galleries to the museum at the Church of Santa Croce, which was flooded to a level of 13 feet.
